source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Deterministic Mediterranean-style lentil soup baseline: olive oil + aromatics + herbs + lemon finish.
Designed to be pantry-friendly and weeknight reliable; supports optional tomato and greens.

ingredients.label: Brown or green lentils, rinsed and picked over
Celery, diced
ingredients.notes: About 2 stalks. Optional but adds classic aromatic depth.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Optional for deeper color and mild sweetness.

ingredients.label: Fine salt
ingredients.notes: Start here; adjust after simmering. (Broth saltiness varies.)

ingredients.label: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: About 1–2 lemons; add to taste at the end.

ingredients.label: Lemon zest
ingredients.notes: Optional but highly recommended for aroma.

ingredients.label: Spinach or chopped kale (optional)
ingredients.notes: Stir in at the end until wilted.

ingredients.label: Red wine vinegar (optional, for extra brightness)

steps.title: Prep and rinse lentils
steps.body: Rinse lentils in a fine-mesh strainer and pick out any stones or debris. Dice onion, carrot (and celery if using). Mince garlic. Juice lemon and chop herbs.
steps.notes: Keeping the dice fairly small helps the soup feel cohesive.

steps.title: Sauté aromatics in olive oil
steps.body: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrot, and celery (optional). Cook 6–8 minutes until softened and lightly glossy. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Bloom spices (and tomato paste if using)
steps.body: Add cumin, coriander (optional), paprika (optional), and tomato paste (optional). Stir 30–45 seconds to bloom spices and coat vegetables.

steps.title: Simmer until lentils are tender
steps.body: Add lentils, broth, salt, pepper (optional), and bay leaf (optional). Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a steady simmer. Cover partially and cook 30–40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender but not mushy.

steps.title: Adjust texture (optional blending)
steps.body: For a creamier soup: remove bay leaf, then blend 1–2 cups of soup and return to the pot (or use an immersion blender for a few quick pulses). For a brothy soup: skip blending. If soup is too thick, add a splash of hot water/broth.
steps.notes: Blending a portion increases body without dairy.

steps.title: Finish with lemon + herbs
steps.body: Turn off heat. Stir in lemon juice (start with half, then add more to taste), lemon zest (optional), parsley, and mint (optional). Add greens (optional) and stir until wilted. Taste and adjust salt/pepper. Add a small splash of red wine vinegar (optional) if you want extra brightness.
steps.notes: Add lemon off heat to keep the flavor fresh and fragrant.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Ladle into bowls. Drizzle with extra olive oil if desired. Serve with pita/flatbread, a crisp salad, or roasted vegetables.

refrigerate.notes: Soup thickens as it sits; add water/broth when reheating.

freeze.notes: Cool completely before freezing. Thaw overnight in fridge; reheat gently.

variations.title: Tomato + lemon lentil soup
changes: Use tomato paste and add 200 g diced tomatoes during simmer.
Increase cumin slightly and add a pinch of oregano.

variations.title: Oregano-forward finish
changes: Add 1–2 g dried oregano during simmer.
Finish with lemon + parsley; skip cumin if you prefer a cleaner herb profile.

variations.title: Spicy version
changes: Add 0.5–1 g red pepper flakes with garlic.
Finish with lemon and a drizzle of olive oil.

notes: Lemon is the signature finish: add it off-heat and adjust gradually.
If using red lentils, reduce simmer time to ~18–25 minutes (they break down more and will be thicker).
Pairs well with: tabbouleh, hummus, roasted_vegetables_olive_oil, or baked fish for a full Mediterranean meal.
